strengths: Three specific, real-world objects are clearly identified with adequate contextual details.
Links between each object and the chosen prompt are explicitly made and generally well explained.
Justifications for each objectâs inclusion demonstrate understanding of how different types of knowledge manifest in practical contexts.
Inclusion of reflective counterpoints and ethical considerations adds depth beyond mere description.
weaknesses: Analysis relies almost exclusively on personal experience and anecdote, lacking external evidence or scholarly perspectives.
Core TOK terminology (e.g. âpersonal knowledgeâ, âshared knowledgeâ, âthreshold conceptsâ) is under-used, reducing conceptual precision.
Some explanations are repetitive or imprecise, and there is no concluding synthesis that unifies insights across all objects.
Clarity is sometimes undermined by overly long sentences and a lack of supporting data for socioeconomic claims.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Awarding 7 marks reflects a solid band-7 performance: the exhibition successfully identifies three well-contextualized objects and makes clear, justified links to the IA prompt. The student shows good TOK understanding and adds valuable ethical reflection. However, the work falls short of the highest bands because it is overly anecdotal, lacks external evidence and TOK terminology, and does not synthesize insights into a cohesive conclusion. These limitations prevent it from reaching band 9â10, but the overall quality merits a strong mid-band score of 7.

annotations.comment: The cello photograph effectively situates the object within its real-world musical context, strengthening the exhibitionâs tangible grounding.
The introductory description of your cello is purely narrative; refocus this section to foreground TOK concepts rather than biographical detail.
You discuss first-hand vs. second-hand knowledge insightfully but omit core TOK terminology such as âpersonal knowledgeâ and âshared knowledge.â
Analysis is grounded exclusively in personal experience; integrating external case studies or scholarly perspectives would enhance academic rigor.
Incorporating a discussion of social and moral responsibilities of knowers adds valuable ethical depth to your TOK exploration.
The moral implications are asserted broadly; specify relevant TOK concepts (e.g., knowledge obligations) to substantiate your ethical claims.
Several sentences run on, reducing clarity; splitting complex ideas into shorter statements will improve precision.
The exhibition lacks a concluding synthesis that ties together insights from all three objects; add a conclusion to unify your argument.
Ensure that each point you make explicitly references the chosen prompt to maintain a clear and consistent focus throughout the exhibition.
No external sources or literature are cited; incorporating research will broaden your perspective and lend academic rigor to your claims.
The student correctly states the chosen prompt, but the introduction could be strengthened by outlining how the three objects will collectively address the question.
Consider defining key terms such as âknowledgeâ at the outset to provide conceptual clarity and frame your exploration of its implications.
Excellent selection of a specific, real-world object (the VEX Triball) with clear photographic context, demonstrating strong grounding in the real-world for TOK analysis.
The explanation relies heavily on a single team example; incorporating external robotics theory or competition data would broaden your evidence and strengthen the argument.
The phrase âknowers make specific subsystemsâ is vague; consider defining types of knowledge (e.g., propositional vs.\\ procedural) to clarify your analysis.
Some sentences are lengthy and repetitive; breaking them into concise points will improve clarity and precision of your analysis.
The acknowledgment of a limitation demonstrates critical reflection, adding nuance by recognizing scenarios where minimal familiarity still yields benefits.
The concept of threshold knowledge is introduced but not fully developed; explicitly link this idea back to the prompt to deepen your reflection.
This discussion of shared knowledge limitations is abstract and lacks concrete examples; tie it back to a real-world scenario for clarity.
The point here repeats earlier limitations; consider merging these insights into a single, cohesive discussion to avoid redundancy.
Choosing the customised badminton racket introduces a valuable socioeconomic dimension, illustrating how access to knowledge can be influenced by wealth.
The descriptive introduction of the racket is too brief; explain why your fatherâs customization is significant to the TOK discussion.
The analysis does not explicitly tie back to the promptâs wording; reinforce links to âimplications of having, or not having, knowledgeâ throughout.
Socioeconomic implications are introduced but unsupported; include data or TOK scholarship to substantiate how wealth affects access to knowledge.
Consider adding a cross-object comparison section to deepen insight by highlighting similarities and differences in how each object illuminates the prompt.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
